steps.title: Identify the requirement for a valuable proxy
steps.content: The text proposes that D. melanogaster could be a “valuable proxy” for X. laevis in testing Ag-NP bioaccumulation. A proxy is valuable only if it reliably predicts the behavior of the species it replaces.

steps.title: Apply the proxy requirement to the choices
steps.content: Choice C directly shows that both species display comparable rates of Ag-NP uptake in comparable environments—precisely what the proxy hypothesis requires. Choices A and B indicate the species respond differently or inconsistently to Ag-NPs, undermining the proxy relationship; choice D discusses detection difficulty rather than whether bioaccumulation patterns align. That matches choice C — C is correct.
